expectations.text: [holdout_regression][blocking] Case focus is explicitly covered: skill improvements for report-editor do not regress a different feature planning flow
expectations.evidence: result.md explicitly states the blocking focus and evaluates cross-feature non-regression: "Improvements for the `report-editor` feature family must not regress a different feature planning flow." It then argues compatibility with the other planning flow (embedding/migration-shell) and lists preserved elements: "Concrete cross-feature elements preserved (non-regression signals) ... toggle/routing structure ... performance section framing ... native integration / menu parity emphasis."

expectations.text: [holdout_regression][blocking] Output aligns with primary phase holdout
expectations.evidence: Both execution_notes.md and result.md constrain themselves to holdout-phase evaluation and provided evidence only: execution_notes.md: "Provided an evidence-based verdict only; did not claim to run phase scripts or generate run artifacts." result.md: "This output is a holdout-phase regression verdict based strictly on provided evidence. It does not attempt to execute phases, spawn subagents, or produce runtime artifacts."
